violations.text: The door to the outside, located at the loading dock, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
The coving along the wall near the steam kettles were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
The meat grip was observed worn down. Grip to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer have an accumulation of food debris. Slicer to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025
The faucet at the food prep sink was observed to be leaking. The plumbing system shall be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025
The floor in the walk-in freezer is in poor repair (metal sheet lifting). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The following areas of physical facilities were observed to be in need of adjustment/repair... - The Floor near the service line area were observed to be chipped/cracked with worn grout lines. - Stainless steel wall coverings were observed to have gaps/possible entry points for pests at wall seams and hardware attachments. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2024

At the time of re-inspection, the following need for cleaning was observed in the kitchen area... - Old rodent droppings and dead insects were observed on the floor - standing water in areas around equipment and broken tiles The physical facilities shall be cleaned and thoroughly dried as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024

The garage door to the outside, located in the loading area, is not tight-fitting (Gap observed to the lower side of the door). The doors to the entrance were also observed with gaps on the bottom of the doors. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The presence of roaches is not controlled as evidenced by observed activity in the kitchen under the unused cooking equipment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment currently has pest control service the building 1x monthly. Establishment recommended to increase pest control visits to 2x monthly until activity is eliminated. Establishment to contact pest control and complete a full evaluation of the building. After evaluation, plan of action to be developed by establishment and sent to inspector by 8/26/24. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The following was observed in poor repair: 1) The metal sheets along the wall in the walk-in cooler was observed with a gap. Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. 2) The pots and pans were observed to be pitted. Broken equipment cannot be properly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024
The Floor near the service line area were observed to be chipped/cracked with worn grout lines. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2024

The floors in the dry storage and kitchen area has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The garage door to the outside, located in the loading area, is not tight-fitting (Gap observed to the lower side of the door). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The following equipment was observed to be in need of repair... - The Hand Sink faucet in the warewashing area was observed to be loose attached - The 3 Bay Sink faucet in the warewashing area was observed to be leaking Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
A Prep Sink in the kitchen area was observed to have a drain and drain stopper lever that was leaking. Plumbing system is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
The Floor near the service line area were observed to be chipped/cracked with worn grout lines. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2024
